observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management facilitate the prompt implementation of
the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-
006 dated January 31, 2020.

We likewise recommended that Management require the:

a. Municipal Treasurer’s Office to maintain the complete and updated details
of the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant, and Equipment
(RCPPE) and Property/Equipment Cards (PECs) to support reconciliation
efforts; and

b. Accounting Office to: (i) individually verify and validate the PPE items
ensuring that only those items that meet the capitalization threshold shall
be recorded as PPE, and refrain from aggregating semi-expendable
properties not purchased in bulk, to determine the correct classification of
the assets; and (ii) prepare and effect the adjusting journal entries to
rectify the identified deficiencies.

We recommended that Management address the observed deficiencies relative to the reconstitution of the Inventory Committee, the institution of an internal control system, and the proper designation of personnel from the Treasury and Accounting Offices to facilitate the implementation of the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.reason: Huge variance still exists between the physical count and accounting records of PPE.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct:

(a)	The OIC - Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer to ensure that SLCs and SCs are maintained, the physical count of inventories is done semestrally, and the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories is submitted, as prescribed in Volume I of MNGAS for LGUs; and

(b)	The Acting Municipal Treasurer to: (i) prepare and submit weekly consolidated Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) together with the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to the OIC - Municipal Accountant to timely record the appropriate expense accounts for the issuance of inventories, as enjoined under Section 121, Volume I of MNGAS for LGUs; and (ii) strictly observe the rules enjoined under Section 118, Volume I of MNGAS for LGUs on the acceptance and inspection of purchased supplies and materials.
observations.managementAction: Conducted the Inventory count but the reconciliation with accounting records is still on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

(a) Issue demand letters to the former Municipal Treasurers to remit in full amounts to the Acting Municipal Treasurer the undeposited collections totaling ₱1,426,065.95; and

(b)	Through the Acting Municipal Treasurer, explain as to what legal basis, the deductions from salaries of the former Municipal Treasurers for the settlement of their undeposited collections, were allowed.
observations.managementAction: No comment was received from the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management facilitate the submission of the certifications required under Paragraph B (5) of COA Resolution No. 2021-044 dated December 28, 2021, to support the payment of claims for travel expenses by the JO/COS personnel.
observations.managementAction: No comment was received from the Management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

(a)	Ensure that MOA is secured first as required under COA Circular No. 94-013, before undertaking projects involving inter-agency fund transfers;

(b)	Instruct the personnel concerned to conduct periodic analysis, review, and monitoring of all fund transfers to ensure compliance with all requirements stipulated in the MOA as well as in other relevant laws and regulations; and

(c)	Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to individually verify and validate the SL account balances relating to various fund transfers to establish rights and obligations therefrom and effect the necessary adjusting journal entries, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in her capacity as the Chairperson of the LDRRMC, ensure proper implementation of the programs, projects, or activities embodied in the LDRRMFIP through strict adherence to the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 10121.
observations.reason: Similar deficiencies were observed for CY 2023

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

(a)	Stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to officers and employees with outstanding cash advances;

(b)	Institute appropriate legal actions/sanctions for non-settlement and non-liquidation of cash advances following the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-004 and other existing laws and regulations, if warranted; and

(c)	Ensure that the granting, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances is in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that Management refrains from disbursing funds through reimbursement by: 

(a) strictly following the imprest fund system; 

(b) planning the procurement activities through the formulation of a comprehensive Annual Procurement Plan; and 

(c) establishing a petty cash fund to be utilized for disbursements involving small amounts for urgent needs of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Similar deficiencies were observed for CY 2023

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct 

(a)  the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to faithfully observe its duties and responsibilities relative to the procurement activities; and 

(b) the Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursement vouchers are supported with complete documentation following COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.reason: Similar deficiencies were noted for CY 2023.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the concerned personnel to:

(a)	Regularly monitor the utilization of the funds so that these are spent for their intended use/activity, and ensure accurate reporting of actual cost in the GAD Accomplishment Report; and 

(b)	Prepare the GAD Accomplishment Report, observing the prescribed procedures of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 (as amended).

We likewise recommended that Management establish and maintain a gender-responsive monitoring and evaluation system by creating a Monitoring and Evaluation Team and/or instructing the GAD Focal Person to use the available monitoring and evaluation tools indicated in the same JMC.
observations.reason: Similar deficiencies were noted for CY 2023
Similar deficiencies were noted for CY 2023
No comment was received from the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management create a special account in the General Fund for the BGCM through an ordinance by the local sanggunian for easy monitoring of the utilization of the Fund and direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and other personnel concerned to prepare the financial statements therefor.

We recommended that Management ensure that procurement of gasoline, oil and lubricants strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 122, Volume I of the MNGAS for the proper sequence of control of inventory particularly in the procurement of said goods, as well as the principle of competitiveness enshrined under Section 3 of R.A. 9184 by extending equal opportunity to enable private contracting parties who are eligible and qualified to participate in a public bidding.

We recommended that Management require the GFPS Executive Committee, with proper coordination of all departments involved, to exert best effort in order to ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the GAD programs, projects and activities and the judicious utilization of the GAD Budget.
observations.reason: Similar deficiency was noted for CY 2023

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the BAC and Municipal Accountant to observe due diligence in the discharge of their functions with regard to the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects, as well as the processing of claims.
observations.reason: The same practice was observed for CY 2023
Similar deficiency was noted for CY 2023

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the concerned accountable officers to deposit/remit undeposited collections immediately; and  

Withhold salaries and benefits of accountable officers with long outstanding undeposited collections or take appropriate legal remedies available against the accountable officers to protect the interest of the government.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the disbursement vouchers together with its complete supporting documents in order that a proper review of transactions can be achieved.
observations.reason: The same deficiency was observed for CY 2023 transactions

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Bids and Award Committee (BAC) to submit promptly all contracts with their supporting documents to the Audit Team for review within five days from perfection thereof in compliance with existing regulations.

We recommended that Management require the responsible officials to:

a)	Prepare PERT/CPM Network Diagram prior to the preparation of the Bidding Documents. The Bids and Awards Committee likewise ensure that the contract time computation be supported with PERT/CPM Network Diagram prepared by the Municipal 
Engineer’s Office; and

b)	Require every contractor of the Municipality’s infrastructure project to prepare an “As-Built” Plan, a new set of drawings accurately depicting each and every part of the structures/facilities as actually built in the completed project and submit the same observing timeliness of submission.

observations.reason: No comment was received from the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere with the rules and regulations in the grant of monetization and/or terminal leave benefits in the procession of claims, and concerned officials to submit the lacking documents to avoid the issuance of Notice of Suspension.
observations.reason: No comment was received from the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to return the remaining undisbursed balance of the BGCM Fund as of December 31, 2021, amounting to ₱25,392.95 to the National Treasury as enjoined under Item 5.0 DBM Circular Letter No. 2021-13 dated December 31, 2021.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Health Office, Treasury Office, and Accounting Office to coordinate with each other to align their inventory control procedures with existing rules and regulations; and facilitate the proper recording of the COVID-19 vaccines and ancillary supplies in the books of accounts as well as in other inventory records.

We recommended that Management require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to:

(a)  update the RPTAR/RPTTIC based on the latest Assessment Roll; and 

(b) furnish the Accounting Office with an updated certified list of taxes due and collectible at the beginning of each year which will serve as the basis for setting up accurate and reliable amounts of RPT/SET Receivables and the related Deferred RPT/Deferred SET Income accounts.

We recommended that Management exhaust all possible remedies to enforce the collection of delinquent taxes especially those prescribed under Sections 256 of RA 7160; or the remedies by administrative action through a levy on real property or by judicial action to lessen the accumulation of tax delinquencies and increase the collection efficiency of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management direct:

(a)	The concerned officers and the LSB to follow the timeline of the preparation and approval of the SEF budget following the relevant period and activities cited in the Joint Circular; and

(b)	The Acting Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to coordinate with each other in the preparation of the certified statement of SEF income to properly establish a more reliable basis of the available funds for subsequent appropriation thereof.

We recommended that Management direct:

(a)	The concerned officers and LSB to strictly adhere to the rules regarding the activities, documents, and timeline of the monitoring of the SEF utilization cited in the Joint Circular and coordinate with each other to facilitate the proper preparation of more reliable and complete records and reports; 

(b)	The OIC-Municipal Accountant to use the appropriate account titles in recording expenses as prescribed in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009; and

(c)	The LSB to refrain from charging from the fund expenses which are not included in the list of allowable expenditures under the SEF provided in the Joint Circular.

We recommended that Management instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:

a)  Prepare and submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements for all existing depository accounts and effect the necessary adjusting entries to record the valid book reconciling items; and

b)  Determine the cause of the negative balance of the Cash in Bank-LCCA of the General Fund-Proper and make necessary corrections to reflect the accurate balance of the said account.

We recommended that Management instruct the Accounting Office to provide depreciation for the identified PPE accounts and effect the necessary adjusting journal entries in the books; take up unrecorded prior years’ depreciation as Prior Years Adjustment to be closed to Government Equity; and record the current year’s depreciation as expense.
observations.managementAction: Depreciations were provided

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that Purchase Orders attached to the disbursement vouchers adhere to the prescribed guidelines on the documentary requirements regarding proper acceptance by the suppliers.

We recommended that Management adhere strictly to the provisions of Section 9.2.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Item V.C.2.b of Annex H of the RIRR of R.A. No. 9184 and ensure that Price Quotations are attached as supporting documents in the procurement of goods through Shopping.

We recommended that Management transfer back the Acting Municipal Treasurer to his previous position as the Municipal Accountant or hire a Certified Public Accountant, if warranted who has the competence and skills to assume the office to facilitate timely submission of the required accounting reports and records which are vital in the audit of the Municipality’s financial transactions.
observations.managementAction: The OIC - Municipal Treasurer returned to his original position as Municipal Account, while a new Municipal Treasurer was appointed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the officers concerned to ensure that a security/guarantee before processing the disbursement voucher for the claim for advance payment/mobilization fee is submitted.

We recommended that Management to strictly adhere to the provisions of R.A. 9003; implement the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan they had prepared; put up receptacles that are well categorized and segregated; take appropriate actions that would lead to the closure of the open dump; and eventually switch to sanitary landfill.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has effected closure of its dumpsite |

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the conditions and requirements set by the rules and regulations for the use of infrastructure project implementation “By Administration.”
observations.reason: No justification was given by the Management

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in his capacity as Chairman of the Local School Board, instruct the personnel concerned to strictly adhere to the schedule of activities in the preparation and approval of SEF Budget observing timeliness in the preparation and submission of the proper documents and records necessary in planning the programs, projects and activities to be charged against the Special Education Fund.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management re-establish and reinforce the deputation of barangay officials to aid the LGU in collecting taxes by setting their specific functions and responsibilities;
observations.reason: No justification was given by the Management
Notice of Disallowance dated June 9, 2017 was issued.  No refund was made by the Management. | No action was made by Management to implement this recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Property/Treasurer’s Office and Accounting Office to:
Comply with the required preliminary activities prior to inventory taking as part of the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts, and henceforth, ensure full implementation of the cleansing process as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; and
Exert efforts to reconcile their records on a regular basis so that discrepancies thereon may be immediately corrected.
The Management was amenable to the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
Prepare and submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements for all existing depository accounts and effect the necessary adjusting entries to record the valid book reconciling items; and
Determine the cause of the negative balance of the Cash in Bank-LCCA of the General Fund-Proper and make necessary corrections to reflect the accurate balance of the said account.
The Management was amenable to the audit recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in his capacity as the Chairman of the LDRRMC, ensure proper implementation of the programs, projects or activities embodied in the LDRRMFIP through strict adherence to the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 10121.
The Management was amenable to the audit recommendation.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Development Council to judiciously plan the programs/ projects/activities (PPAs) to be funded out of the 20 per cent Development Fund and allocate the Fund to a reasonable number of PPAs or only those of utmost priority which can be probably undertaken within the year, taking into account the estimated amount of time spent for the procurement and all other factors affecting the implementation process; and
Advise the Municipal Engineer to intensify control and supervision over the infrastructure projects, conduct frequent inspection of all municipal works and regularly monitor the status of all projects in order to immediately address causes of failure or delay in the implementation thereof.
Management Comment:
The Municipal Engineer expressed commitment to comply with the audit recommendations as far as the infrastructure projects are concerned. However, he justified that most of the unimplemented local development PPAs pertains to agriculture and coastal resource management programs which are under the responsibility and supervision of the Municipal Agriculture Office.

We recommended that Management:
Prepare and issue demand letters to concerned officers and employees as well as non-LGU employees with outstanding cash advances requiring them to immediately settle their unliquidated cash advances;
Institute appropriate legal actions/sanctions for non-settlement and non-liquidation of cash advances in accordance to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-004 and other existing laws and regulations, if warranted;
Refrain from granting additional cash advances to the Disbursing Officer prior to the settlement or liquidation of her previous cash advances; and
Ensure that the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances is in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002.
Management Comment:
The OIC-Municipal Treasurer was amenable to the audit recommendations.  However, he justified that the remaining balance of cash advances pertains to the prior years’ records or those transactions handled by the previous Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that Management instruct the Accounting Office to provide depreciation for the identified PPE accounts and effect the necessary adjusting journal entries in the books; take up unrecorded prior years’ depreciation as Prior Years Adjustment to be closed to Government Equity; and record the current year’s depreciation as expense.
The Management was amenable to the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management ensure that Purchase Orders attached to the disbursement vouchers adhere to the prescribed guidelines on the documentary requirements regarding proper acceptance by the suppliers.
The Management was amenable to the audit recommendation.

We recommended that Management ensure that procurement of gasoline, oil and lubricants strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 122, Volume I of the MNGAS for the proper sequence of control of inventory particularly in the procurement of said goods, as well as the principle of competitiveness enshrined under Section 3 of R.A. 9184 by extending equal opportunity to enable private contracting parties who are eligible and qualified to participate in a public bidding.
The Management was amenable to the audit recommendation.

We recommended that Management adhere strictly to the provisions of Section 9.2.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Item V.C.2.b of Annex H of the RIRR of R.A. No. 9184 and ensure that Price Quotations are attached as supporting documents in the procurement of goods through Shopping.
The Management was amenable to the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management require the responsible officials to:
Prepare PERT/CPM Network Diagram prior to the preparation of the Bidding Documents. The Bids and Awards Committee likewise ensure that the contract time computation be supported with PERT/CPM Network Diagram prepared by the Municipal Engineer’s Office; and
Require every contractor of the Municipality’s infrastructure project to prepare an “As-Built” Plan, a new set of drawings accurately depicting each and every part of the structures/facilities as actually built in the completed project and submit the same observing timeliness of submission.
Management Comment:
The Municipal Engineer justified that the PERT-CPM Network Diagrams were prepared but were not attached to the disbursement vouchers. Nevertheless, he promised to comply with the audit recommendations to prepare and submit the PERT/CPM Network Diagram prior to the preparation of the Bidding Documents, as well as to require every contractor of the infrastructure project to prepare an “As-Built” Plan.

We recommended that Management instruct the officers concerned to ensure that a security/guarantee before processing the disbursement voucher for the claim for advance payment/mobilization fee is submitted.
The Management was amenable to the audit recommendation.

We recommended that Management require the GFPS Executive Committee, with proper coordination of all departments involved, to exert best effort in order to ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the GAD programs, projects and activities and the judicious utilization of the GAD Budget.
The Management promised to comply with the audit recommendation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in his capacity as Chairman of the Local School Board, facilitate the timely preparation and approval of the SEF Budget, strictly adhering to the schedule of activities prescribed under Section 5.0 of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular (JC) No. 1 s. 2017 (as addended).
The Management was amenable to the audit recommendation.

We recommended that Management direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to ensure that checks will only be prepared and issued once the correct details were already on hand before printing thereon to prevent committing errors in the preparation of checks and to warrant the reliability of data processing and payments, consequently safeguarding the Municipality’s resources from loss or wastage.
The Management was amenable to the audit recommendation.

We recommended that Management direct the BAC and Municipal Accountant to observe due diligence in the discharge of their functions with regard to the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects, as well as the processing of claims.
The Management was amenable to the audit recommendation.

We recommended that Management instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:

Submit BRS for the SEF and Trust Fund and effect the necessary adjusting entries to record the valid book reconciling items for all the funds;

Determine the cause of the negative balance of the Cash in Bank-LCCA of the General Fund-Proper and make necessary corrections to reflect the accurate balance of the said account; and
Maintain separate bank accounts for GF- Proper and 20% Development Fund for easier monitoring of the cash in bank balances.
observations.managementAction: Preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statement for year 2021 is on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count and prepare the RPCPPE properly, including maintenance of Property Cards and ensure that RPCPPE contains adequate details such as description, property number, remarks on the condition and whereabouts of PPE and other information necessary to facilitate identification and reconciliation of records;

Direct the Accounting Office to maintain updated PPE Ledger Cards in order to completely account for each item composing the PPE, investigate on the discrepancies noted and effect the necessary adjustments in the books; and
Designate on a full-time basis at least one personnel each from the Property and Accounting Offices to reconcile the Report on the Physical Count of PPE, Property Cards, Property Ledger Cards, and accounting records, taking into consideration the noted deficiencies in audit. A cut-off date as of December 31, 2020 may be started to facilitate current reconciliation, then move back to earlier years.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee already conducted inventory count of all PPE but reconciliation is on-going.

observations.reason: There is a designated stockroom. However, the Management immediately distributes all supplies to all end-users once the suppliers delivered the goods. There is no inventory logbook maintained by the Inventory Committee due to the lack of a full-time General Services Committee. There are no subsidiary ledger cards and subsidiary ledgers due to the lack of expertise of concerned personnel. The Management commits to comply with the recommendations.

observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee already conducted inventory count but reconciliation is on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management exert effort in limiting their PS expenses by identifying and funding only items that are of priority concerns so that adequate funds can be allocated for the implementation of other planned development projects beneficial to the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The LGU complied with the prescribed PS Limitation for CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Chairman of the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly observe the proper posting of advertisements in the PhilGEPS as required under the RIRR of R.A. 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the disbursement vouchers together with their complete supporting documents so that proper review of transactions can be achieved, without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance, if warranted, as a result of post-audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management transfer back the Acting Municipal Treasurer to his previous position as the Municipal Accountant or hire a Certified Public Accountant, if warranted who has the competence and skills to assume the office to facilitate timely submission of the required accounting reports and records which are vital in the audit of the Municipality’s financial transactions.

The current OIC-Municipal Treasurer who is a CPA will be returned to his original position as Municipal Accountant once a new Municipal Treasurer is appointed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the Municipal Development Council to judiciously plan the programs/projects/activities to be funded out of the 20% DF in accordance with the mandate of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1; and

Ensure that all DF projects proposed in the Annual Investment Program of the LGU be fully implemented within the budget year.
observations.reason: The MDC always plans the PPAs under the 20% LDF. It prioritizes according to the necessity and urgency of PPAs. However, the LDF PPAs can hardly be fully implemented within the budget year due to the long timeframe involved before they can be finished since they are almost entirely infrastructure PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that the realignment of 20% DF to entirely new PPAs shall effect changes in the Annual Budget through enactment of an ordinance authorizing a supplemental budget in accordance with Section 321 of Local Government Code.
observations.managementAction: Realignments made during CY 2021 were incorporated in a supplemental budget.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
Submit BRS for the SEF and Trust Fund and effect the necessary adjusting entries to record the valid book reconciling items for all the funds;
Determine the cause of the negative balance of the Cash in Bank-LCCA of the General Fund-Proper and make necessary corrections to reflect the accurate balance of the said account; and
Maintain separate bank accounts for GF- Proper and 20% Development Fund for easier monitoring of the cash in bank balances.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count and prepare the RPCPPE properly, including maintenance of Property Cards and ensure that RPCPPE contains adequate details such as description, property number, remarks on the condition and whereabouts of PPE and other information necessary to facilitate identification and reconciliation of records;
Direct the Accounting Office to maintain updated PPE Ledger Cards in order to completely account for each item composing the PPE, investigate on the discrepancies noted and effect the necessary adjustments in the books; and
Designate on a full-time basis at least one personnel each from the Property and Accounting Offices to reconcile the Report on the Physical Count of PPE, Property Cards, Property Ledger Cards, and accounting records, taking into consideration the noted deficiencies in audit. A cut-off date as of December 31, 2020 may be started to facilitate current reconciliation, then move back to earlier years.

We recommended that Management instruct:
The Inventory Committee to conduct an inventory of the local roads under the jurisdiction of the municipality and render a Report on the Physical count of the Road Networks System;
The Acting Municipal Treasurer (where no General Services Officer was designated) to;
Render a Report on Local Road Network and Road Map,
Regularly maintain and update the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads,
Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components;
The Municipal Engineer to provide the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; and
The OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
Prepare the JEV to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components,
Prepare and maintain a Local Road Network Ledger Card,
Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network, and
Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

We recommended that Management exert effort in limiting their PS expenses by identifying and funding only items that are of priority concerns so that adequate funds can be allocated for the implementation of other planned development projects beneficial to the constituents.
We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the conditions and requirements set by the rules and regulations for the use of infrastructure project implementation “By Administration.”
We recommended that Management instruct the Chairman of the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly observe the proper posting of advertisements in the PhilGEPS as required under the RIRR of R.A. 9184.

We recommended that Management create a special account in the General Fund for the BGCM through an ordinance by the local sanggunian for easy monitoring of the utilization of the Fund and direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and other personnel concerned to prepare the financial statements therefor.

We recommended that Management:
Ensure that the ability of a supplier to meet the requirements of the procurement is properly evaluated pursuant to the provisions of RA No. 9184 on eligibility requirements of prospective bidders/suppliers; and
Direct the personnel concerned to hasten the submission of the list of recipients of the distributed food supplies.

We recommended that Management refrain from disbursing funds out of the BGCM for expenditures not allowed under DBM LBC No. 125 and direct the persons concerned to facilitate the immediate submission of the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents for expenditures of the Bayanihan Grant.

We recommended that Management ensure that the disbursements of the LDRRMF only pertain to programs, projects and activities judiciously planned and indicated in details in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Investment Plan which should also include under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged against the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years.

We recommended that Management require the GFPS Executive Committee to exert best effort in order to ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the GAD programs, projects and activities and the judicious utilization of the GAD Budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in his capacity as Chairman of the Local School Board, instruct the personnel concerned to strictly adhere to the schedule of activities in the preparation and approval of SEF Budget observing timeliness in the preparation and submission of the proper documents and records necessary in planning the programs, projects and activities to be charged against the Special Education Fund.

We recommended that Management direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the disbursement vouchers together with their complete supporting documents so that proper review of transactions can be achieved, without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/ Disallowance, if warranted, as a result of post-audit.

We recommended that Management transfer back the Acting Municipal Treasurer to his previous position as the Municipal Accountant or hire a Certified Public Accountant, if warranted who has the competence and skills to assume the office to facilitate timely submission of the required accounting reports and records which are vital in the audit of the Municipality’s financial transactions.

Require the concerned accountable officers to deposit/remit undeposited collections immediately; and  

Withhold salaries and benefits of accountable officers with long outstanding undeposited collections or take appropriate legal remedies available against the accountable officers to protect the interest of the government.

Designate a regular employee each from property and accounting offices who shall update and prepare the respective property records of all items of PPE on a full-time basis.

Require the Municipal Development Council to judiciously plan the programs/projects/ activities to be funded out of the 20% EDF in accordance with the mandate of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1; and

observations.reason: Partially Implemented | Infrastructure projects from CYs 2015 to 2018 were fully implemented. For CY 2019, 27 out of 32  infrastructure projects were fully implemented, while 5 projects are ongoing but temporarily stopped due to right of way problems and non-delivery of local materials. For Economic Services Sector, projects are partially implemented due to implementing office problem.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Bids and Award Committee (BAC) to submit promptly all contracts with their supporting documents to the Audit Team for review within five days from perfection thereof in compliance with existing regulations.

Direct the Municipal Engineer to require the completion of the projects as soon as possible and if applicable, intensify the monitoring of project implementation to ensure that projects are completed within the period indicated in the contract; and

Revisit the contract agreement and compute for liquidated damages for negative slippage and/or rescind the contract in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 9184.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of all inter-agency payables in order to establish the correctness and accuracy of the accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:


      a)  require  the  concerned  accountable  officers  to   deposit/remit
         undeposited collections immediately; and

      b) withhold salaries and benefits of accountable  officers  with  long
         outstanding  undeposited  collections  or  take  appropriate  legal
         remedies available against the accountable officers to protect  the
         interest of the government.

We recommended that Management:


     a) Stop the granting of  additional  cash  advances  to  officers  and
        employees unless the previous ones are fully settled;


     b) Institute appropriate legal actions/sanctions against those  erring
        accountable officers/employees for  continued  non-compliance  with
        existing laws, rules and regulations on the  granting,  liquidation
        and utilization of cash advances, if warranted; and

     c) Ensure that the  granting,  utilization  and  liquidation  of  cash
        advances is in accordance with the provisions of COA  Circular  No.
        97-002.

We recommended that Management:


      a. create a committee composed of  one  regular  personnel  each  from
         property and accounting who shall reconcile on a  full-time  basis,
         the submitted Report of  Physical  Count  of  Property,  Plant  and
         Equipment (RPCPPE) with the  accounting  records  to  identify  any
         discrepancies that may require adjustments; and

      b. designate a regular employee  each  from  property  and  accounting
         offices who  shall  update  and  prepare  the  respective  property
         records of all items of PPE on a full-time basis.

We recommended that Management  require  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to
immediately return the unutilized funds to the  NGAs  concerned  or  request
for appropriate authority to use said funds.

We recommended that Management direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to
submit immediately the disbursement  vouchers  together  with  its  complete
supporting documents in order that a proper review of  transactions  can  be
achieved.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Management to direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the disbursement vouchers together with its complete supporting documents in order that a proper review of transactions can be achieved.
observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Management to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the bank reconciliation statements and thereafter submit copies thereof to the Audit Team for verification.
observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Management to: a.) require the concerned accountable officers to deposit/remit their undcposited/unrcmitted collections immediately;, and b.) withhold the salaries and benefits of accountable officers with long outstanding undeposited collections or take appropriate legal remedies available against the accountable officers to protect the interest of the government.
observations.reason: Restated in Part I of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Management to direct the Inventory Committee and Municipal Accountant to conduct physical inventory count, reconcile inventory and accounting records and submit the RPCI as required by Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs.
observations.reason: The Management did not create Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory count, reconcile inventory and submit the RPCI as required.

observations.recommendation: We  recommend the Management to comply with the 55 percent personal services limitation in accordance with RA 7160 such that disbursements shall be limited to the amount of appropriation as approved and reviewed in the annual budget.
observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to adhere strictly to the recommendations/exceptions noted by the reviewing office to forestall the issuance of Notices of Disallowances.
observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Management to (a) adhere to the provisions of the abovementioned COA Circular regarding the accounting of LDRRMF; (b) direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that full disclosure of the amount and details of the unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF in the Notes. to FS of the Municipality at year-end and a Trust Liability-DRRM be established; (c) fully implement the plan through maximum utilization of funds to further enhance its disaster preparedness; and (d) stop charging the payment of honorarium of various employees to calamity fund.
observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Management to return the unused PDAF amounting to P1,238,936.00 to the National Treasury in compliance to the Supreme Court Decision No. G.R. No. 208566.
observations.reason: The Management 2015 did not refund the unused PDAF.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Management to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the fund liquidation report pertaining to the completed projects in order that the account of the Municipality in the books of the source agency is settled.
observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Management to (a) strictly adhere to the provisions of R.A. 9003; (b) implement the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan they had prepared; (c) put up receptacles that are well categorized and segregated; (c) take appropriate actions that would lead to the closure of the open dump; and (d) eventually switch fo sanitary landfill.
observations.reason: No Management justification.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Management to provide the oversight agencies copies of the approved Annual GAD Plan and Budget and the Accomplishment Report on GAD to comply with the requirements of the above mentioned provision.
observations.reason: No Management justification.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Management to require the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the submission of the accounts at the earliest possible time in order that a proper review of transactions can be achieved.
We recommend the Management to implement the projects funded from the 20% Local Development Fund so that the constituents can benefit from - positive outcome that may be derived therefrom.
observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:

(a) facilitate the preparation and/or submission of the monthly bank
reconciliation statements; and

(b) prepare the necessary adjustments to record the reconciling items
affecting the accounts.

We recommended that Management:

(a) stop the granting of additional cash advances to Accountable Officers
and employees unless the previous ones are fully settled;

(b) institute appropriate legal actions/sanctions against erring accountable
officers/employees for continued non-compliance with the existing laws,
rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash
advances, if warranted; and

(c) ensure that the granting utilization and liquidation of cash advances is in
accordance with the provision of COA Circular No. 97-602.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to submit immediately the disbursement vouchers together with its
complete supporting documents in order that a proper review of transactions can be
achieved.

We recommended that Management:

(a) require the concerned accountable officers to deposit/remit undeposited
collections immediately; and

(b) withhold salaries and benefits of accountable officers with long outstanding
undeposited collections or take appropriate legal remedies available against
the accountable officers to protect the interest of the government.

We recommended that Management to:

(a) create an Inventory Committee to facilitate the inventory count of ali the
LGU’s assets; and

(b) reconcile the report with the accounting records to identify any
discrepancies that may require adjustment.

We recommended that Management comply with the 55 percent personal
services limitation in accordance with RA 7160 such that disbursements shall be
limited to the amount of appropriation as approved and reviewed in the annual
budget.

We recommended that Management require:

a) the refund of the amount of £308,000.00 without prejudice to the
institution  of  appropriate legal  sanctions  against erring
officials/employees;

b} demand explanation from the AGDB why said checks were encashed
and/or debited from the LGU account without the Accountant’s Advise
otherwise hold the ADGB liable for the loss;

¢) that all claims against LGU funds must be supported with complete
documents appearing legal, valid and proper;

d) strictly observe fiscal responsibility to avoid loss or wastage of
government funds; and

€) the change of authorized depository bank from PVB to LBP or DBP in
order to preserve the interest of the Municipality, otherwise request.
authority from DOF.

‘We recommended that Management direct the Accounting Office to trace the
difference and reconcile the reciprocal accounts, and thereafter effect adjustments
to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable data on its financial
operation as of a given period of time.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the balances of sll inter-agency
payables in order to establish the correctness and accuracy of the accounts.

We  recommended that Management  adhere strictly to the

recommendations/exceptions noted by the reviewiag office to forestall the issuance
of Notices of Disallowances.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the: (a) Municipal
Accountant to strictly adhere with the rules and regulations in the grant of
monetization and/or terminal leave benefits in the processing of claims; and (b)
concerned officials to submit the lacking documents to avoid the issnance of Notice
of Suspension.

We recommended that Management submit the liquidation reports for the
fund transfers and/or refund the unatilized balances for completed projects.

We recommended that Management:

(a) strictly adhere to RA 3720 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the
documentary requirements in the procurement of drugs and medicines
before effecting any payments thereof; and

(b) require the immediate submission of the necessary documents for
transactions already paid to forestall the issuance of Notice of Suspension.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

(a) adhere to the provisions of the abovementioned Circular regarding the
utilization of LDRRMTF; and

(b) direct the Municipal Accountant tn‘ ensure full disclosure of the amount and
details of the unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF in the Notes
to FS of the Muuicipality at year-end.

To promptly implement 2l the programs/projects/activities as embodied in
the approved Annual Development Plan in order to optimize the delivery of basic
services to the people, we recommended that Management:

(a) require the Municipal Development Council to judiciously plan the
programs/projects/activities to be fanded out of the 20% EDF in accerdance
with the mandate of DILG-DBM JMC Ne. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011; and

(b.) prosecute the projects through straight comtract with terms and
conditions indicating definite date of completion.

We recommend the Management to: a) create an Inventory Team to conduct complete physical count of PPE and submit the required RPCPPE as required under Section 124 of Manual on NGAS for LGUs; and b) require the General Service Office and Accounting Office to maintain PLC and PPELC respectively.
observations.reason: Restated in Part I of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Management to refrain from paying prior year’s expenses unless they were obligated in the same year and recorded as accounts payable or any appropriate payable accounts when due and demandable, to comply with the above-mentioned provisions.

We recommend the Management to (a) require the Municipal Accountant to monitor the deductions and remittances to the GSIS in order to avoid penalties/sanctions; and (2) ensure that government funds shall not be used to pay for the interest incurred due to delayed remittance because any penalties/surcharges than may be incurred due to delayed remittances of premiums shall be for the account of the employee who caused the delay in the payment of premiums.
